methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Precision AI is a Series A stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The target has raised $20M to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 8 private companies in drones and autonomous systems, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air; no direct public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Valuation triangulation relied on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-Progression analysis applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $20M total capital raised, treating this as a proxy for post-seed valuation. This methodology is standard for early-stage venture and yielded an implied valuation of $40M. Peer-Median analysis was not applicable: while 5 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier produced fewer than 3 comparable datapoints, making any median calculation statistically unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $34M to $46M, with a median estimate of $40M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty of single-method triangulation; the upper and lower bounds approximate ±15% sensitivity around the point estimate. The principal constraint is the absence of sufficiently similar private comparables at Series A in this emerging subsector. Resolution of this uncertainty would require either identification of additional same-stage peers with disclosed valuations or demonstration of material revenue or customer traction differentiating Precision AI within the peer cohort.
